title: Frontier results, on device - RL Nabors, Arize
summary: RL Nabors argues that most applications unnecessarily default to frontier models at high cost in latency, energy, and money when small local models would suffice. The talk provides vocabulary for model evaluation (capability evals, golden datasets, LLM-as-judge) and a framework for deciding when to use small local models versus frontier models, supported by production-trace-based eval examples.
writeup: RL Nabors of Arize makes the case that many applications default to frontier models even though small local models would meet the requirement, paying unnecessary costs in latency, energy, and money. The talk lays out vocabulary for model evaluation, including capability evals, golden datasets, and LLM-as-judge, and presents a framework for deciding between small local models and frontier models. The argument is supported by eval examples built on production traces.
